title: Private control-plane access: directory provisioning & role mapping handoff
preview:
From: John O'Neil <john.oneil@bigbank.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
Cc: itsec@bigbank.com
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 16:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Private control-plane access - initial ask

Hi Hannah,

Thanks for the call today. To summarize our requirements from Big Bank's security team: we need the private control plane (VPC-hosted) to integrate with our Okta tenant for SAML-based SSO and SCIM provisioning. Key asks:

- Support for federated AWS role assumption from Okta (we prefer role chaining so we can require MFA at the IdP).
- SCIM provisioning for users + groups (group membership should drive RBAC roles on the control plane).
- A canonical mapping of Okta groups -> Redwood control-plane roles for audit.
- Short-lived service credentials and clear rotation guidance for any service accounts.

We'd like a technical handoff doc and sample role mapping CSV so our infra team can run tests in a staging VPC. Attached is the internal security questionnaire excerpt that outlines network constraints.

Can your team supply the SCIM attribute names, SAML metadata (Entity ID/ACS), and a recommended service-account pattern?

Best,
John
IT Security, Big Bank

---
Attachment: bigbank-security-excerpt.pdf (pdf, 32KB)

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 09:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Private control-plane access - initial ask

Team,

Looping you in on Big Bank's ask (see John O'Neil's note below). Requested deliverables for the handoff:

1) SAML metadata snippet (EntityID + ACS + cert thumbprint) we expose for their IdP.
2) SCIM provisioning contract: endpoint, auth (Bearer token format), supported attributes and group schema.
3) Canonical CSV mapping (Okta group -> control-plane role) + example provisioning sync.
4) Recommended service-account pattern for the control-plane gateway (least privilege, rotation cadence).
5) Playbook for staging tests (create user, assign group -> verify role mapping, token expiry).

I've scheduled a 30m handoff slot with John & Maria (Okta partner) on Thu 12/3 at 10am PT. Please prepare the artifacts above and point out any gaps.

Dev: can you draft the SCIM contract and role-mapping CSV?
Rafael: can you write the playbook steps for staging verification and any AWS role mapping notes?
Kimberly: please review the security appetite and sign off on secrets/rotation guidance.

Thanks,
Hannah
Sr. Product Manager, Private Deployments
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: meeting-invite.ics (ics)

From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 11:40:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Private control-plane access - initial ask

Hannah / team,

Draft notes (quick tech summary) — happy to expand into a formal SCIM doc: 

SAML:
- We will provide an SP metadata XML: entityID=https://controlplane.private.redwood.ai/sp, ACS=https://controlplane.private.redwood.ai/saml/acs, cert (PEM).
- Okta will be configured as IdP with assertion containing Subject/NameID (email) and an attribute 'roles' if they want to pass explicit role claims. We recommend mapping groups to SAML attributes for auditability.

SCIM:
- SCIM endpoint hosted on control-plane private ingress: POST/PUT/DELETE /scim/v2/Users and /scim/v2/Groups.
- Auth: Bearer token (rotatable, short TTL), delivered over TLS. Token issuance: customer creates a SCIM-service token via our admin UI or via a KMS-backed provisioning flow in Private deployments.
- Required attributes we consume: userName (email), givenName, familyName, active (boolean), externalId. For groups: displayName, members (value: user externalId).
- Group -> Role mapping: CSV with columns [okta_group_name, controlplane_role, enforced_mfa (true/false), comment]. Example attached.

AWS role mapping guidance (for customers running the control plane in AWS):
- Use SAML federation to AWS: create an IAM role with a trust policy allowing Okta's SAML provider to assume the role. We recommend a dedicated "controlplane-operator" role per account with minimal privileges (attach managed policies for read/write only to RBAC-managed resources).
- For cross-account access (management/staging/production), prefer separate roles per environment and use Okta group-based session tags to pass environment context.

Service account pattern:
- controlplane-gateway-sa: scoped to listing & pushing tokens to the KV store; secrets rotated via KMS every 30 days.
- Avoid long-lived static credentials; prefer short-lived minting via STS where possible.

I'll attach a CSV sample and a short checklist.

-Dev

---
Attachment: redwood-role-mapping-canonical.csv (csv)
Attachment: scim-provisioning-checklist.xlsx (xlsx, 14KB)

From: Maria Gomez <maria.gomez@okta-partners.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, John O'Neil <john.oneil@bigbank.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Kim Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 02 Dec 2026 08:07:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Private control-plane access - initial ask

Hi all,

Thanks for the notes — a couple items from Okta side that often cause friction:

1) SCIM connector expectations: Okta expects a /Scim/Users endpoint and will send POST/PUT operations with schemas that include 'externalId' and 'profile' nested attributes. Confirm you will accept 'schemas' array and tolerate unknown attributes (best practice).
2) Token format: Okta will store a Bearer token (we recommend at least 128-bit entropy). If you support scoped tokens (e.g., scim:write, scim:read) it's easier to audit.
3) Group sync: Okta can push group membership changes as delta-syncs; ensure your implementation supports membership Add/Remove semantics within Groups resource.
4) SAML cert rollover: ask customers to configure multiple certificate entries in Okta to avoid outage during rollover.

I'll join the handoff on Thu to walk through the Okta admin flows with John. Attached is a short troubleshooting matrix we give customers.

Cheers,
Maria Gomez
Okta Partner Solutions Engineer

---
Attachment: okta-scim-troubleshooting.pdf (pdf, 8KB)

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: John O'Neil <john.oneil@bigbank.com>, Maria Gomez <maria.gomez@okta-partners.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 02 Dec 2026 11:15:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Private control-plane access - initial ask

John/Maria,

Thanks for the quick turn. Attached are the canonical CSV and a short playbook for your team to run in staging tomorrow: create user, assign Okta group, verify SCIM propagation, then verify role mapping on control plane UI and API. Rafael has flagged the AWS role mapping snippet for the meeting.

Next steps:
- Thu 12/03 10:00am PT: Handoff call (30m) — we'll demo the SCIM sync and show the SAML metadata import.
- Big Bank: perform the staging checklist and share logs (SCIM response codes + SAML test assertions).

If there are any blockers today, call my cell.

Hannah
Sr. Product Manager, Private Deployments
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: controlplane-access-playbook.pdf (pdf, 22KB)

title: Request: timebound operator ledger package for SOC 2/SIG - MedicaSys
Billing anchor, minimum floors, and pilot concession for BrightMart
preview:
From: Jordan Li <jordan.li@brightmart.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-28T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Billing units for long-context workflows

Hi Camila,

Thanks for the demo yesterday — our engineering team has one outstanding question before we can move to procurement: 

- How does Redwood account tokens for hybrid calls where we send a 150k-character context (approx. 40k tokens) and then stream short completions? Specifically: do you round up per-call to the next 1k token increment, or do you charge exact tokens with a monthly minimum? Our infra team is worried about unexpectedly large bills if every long-context call gets rounded.

Also, can we get a short written example of how rounding/minimums would apply to a weekly batch of ~2,000 such calls (peak/avg patterns included)? If you have a sample invoice or workbook that shows the math that would help.

Jordan Li
Head of AI Engineering
BrightMart

Attachments: none

From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Li <jordan.li@brightmart.com>
Date: 2026-11-28T13:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Billing units for long-context workflows

Jordan — thanks, great question. Short answer: we avoid per-call inflation by defaulting to exact token accounting at ingest + output, with an optional \"anchor floor\" model for committed customers to prevent variability. Details below and I attached a worked example workbook.

Key points:
- Token accounting: we charge for actual input tokens + output tokens. We tokenize at request time using the same tokenizer as the serving model (so you see identical numbers).
- Rounding: there is no system-level per-call 1k rounding for hosted pay-as-you-go. Rounding only applies when a contract has a minimum floor (e.g., monthly committed units) or for certain legacy pilot SKUs that still used quantum buckets.
- Anchor floor (what we propose for BrightMart): we set a monthly committed token floor (e.g., 2.5M tokens) and bill actual usage up to that floor. Above the floor you pay actual tokens. This prevents invoice spikes while still keeping unit economics explicit.

Worked example (see attached workbook):
- Weekly batch: 2k calls, each ~40k tokens input + 250 tokens avg output = 40,250 tokens/call -> 80.5M tokens/week.
- Without a commitment: you would be billed exact tokens for that week. No per-call rounding.
- With a 100M/month floor: your weekly usage would be credited against the floor; in this scenario, some weeks will use the whole floor and others will draw beyond it — smoothing happens at the monthly invoice level, not per-call.

Proposed pilot concession (to unblock procurement):
- 3-month pilot with a 50M/month anchor (floor) instead of a full-commit 100M. Any usage above the anchor is billed at list minus 10% pilot discount. At the end of pilot we reconvene on actuals and term.

I attached: proposed-commit-workbook.xlsx (breakdown by call, weekly/monthly rollups) and pricing_anchor_note.pdf (one-page explanation).

If helpful, I can loop in our billing operations lead (Tessa) and Rafael (sales lead) to approve the pilot parameters.

—Camila
Camila Reyes
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference

Attachments: proposed-commit-workbook.xlsx, pricing_anchor_note.pdf

From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
To: Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Li <jordan.li@brightmart.com>
Date: 2026-11-29T10:20:00-08:00
Subject: FW: Billing units for long-context workflows — pilot concession ask

Tessa / Rafael — forwarding BrightMart's ask plus my recommended pilot concession above. TL;DR: they want a temporary lower anchor (50M/mo) for 3 months; we bill actuals above anchor at a 10% pilot discount. Workbook attached has weekly call examples and a sample invoice line. Can you confirm internal approval and any operational notes we need (reporting cadence, overage notification thresholds, invoice line item conventions)?

Excerpt from workbook (table snippet):
- Calls/week: 2,000
- Avg tokens/call: 40,250
- Tokens/week: 80,500,000
- Tokens/month (4.33 wks): ~348,665,000
- Anchor request: 50,000,000 tokens/month
- Expected overage (month 1): 298,665,000 tokens billed at pilot rate (list - 10%)

Operational asks for billing ops:
- Can billing show monthly reconciliation with line items: anchor credit (50M), billed tokens at list/pilot rate, and a short per-call sample row for audit?
- Are there anti-abuse floor protections we should add (to prevent synthetic token inflation through repeated long prefixes)? We don't want BrightMart to feel they need to throttle.

Thanks — once we have your ok I will send a short commercial term email for signature and route DocuSign.

—Camila

Attachments: proposed-commit-workbook.xlsx, pricing_anchor_note.pdf

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Li <jordan.li@brightmart.com>
Date: 2026-11-30T08:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: FW: Billing units for long-context workflows — pilot concession ask

Looks reasonable to me. Approving the 3-month 50M/month anchor with the overage at list -10% as a pilot concession. Tessa — please confirm we can produce the monthly reconciliation (anchor credit + per-call sample rows) and that we add the following protective language:
- We reserve the right to flag and review anomalous tokenization patterns indicative of synthetic inflation; any confirmed abuse will trigger an invoice adjustment.

If ops is good, Camila — proceed to send the short commercial term email and DocuSign link. I'll backfill any required sign-off internally.

—Rafael
VP, Sales, Redwood Inference

Attachments: none

From: Jordan Li <jordan.li@brightmart.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-30T16:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: FW: Billing units for long-context workflows — pilot concession ask

Thanks, everyone — this sounds good and should unblock procurement. We'll sign the 3-month pilot with the 50M/mo anchor and overage at list -10%. Please send the DocuSign and the sample invoice format so our finance team has the reconciliation rows.

One more ask: can you include a short note in the DocuSign about how token counts were calculated (tokenizer used + seed example)? That helps our auditors.

Jordan Li
Head of AI Engineering, BrightMart

Attachments: DocuSign request pending (link: https://na0.docusign.com/sign/EXAMPLEID123)

title: Persistent chunked-transfer / WebSocket timeouts — BrightLeaf production impact
preview:
From: Megan Lowe <megan@brightleaf.com>
To: rafael.mendes@redwood.ai
Cc: ops@brightleaf.com
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Production realtime feed intermittent timeouts (BrightLeaf)

Hi Rafael,

We started seeing surges of 502/504s on our realtime websocket connections to Redwood around 07:30 PST this morning. The symptom looks like clients receiving partial chunks then a connection close; retries spike downstream. Impact: our realtime dashboards fall behind (up to ~30s) and our customer-facing chat has degraded — error rate up ~12% on the streaming API and p50->p99 latency has widened ~3x.

Date: Mon, 29 Nov 2026 16:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Production realtime feed intermittent timeouts (BrightLeaf) — root cause and rollout plan

Team — quick technical summary for the exec note:
Hypothesis: a bursty downstream worker caused a transient backlog in our streaming dispatcher, which triggered buffer exhaustion at the proxy layer and produced premature connection resets (partial chunk transmission followed by close). Confidence: medium-high (correlated traces + queue depth metrics).

Proposed immediate plan (non-invasive):
1) Keep the increased edge buffer in place (safe for 72 hours) — reduces early TCP/TLS resets and should cut client-visible partial-chunk closes by ~70% based on local repro.
2) Deploy a conservative rate limiter in the dispatcher to smooth backend fan-out (can be rolled in 30m with canary).
3) Post-rollout: enable additional observability flags and a short-lived sampling of full payload traces for BrightLeaf tenant.

Risks: rate limiter may add modest tail latency for high-throughput bursts (we'll canary at 10% traffic and monitor p95/p99).

If agreed, Hannah will push the rate-limiter canary tonight and I'll prepare the rollback script. Also attaching a timeline screenshot summarizing the spike (conn-timeline-11-29.png).

title: Observability add-ons pricing workshop: retention, tracing, dashboards
preview:
From: Elena Morris <elena.morris@cardinalhealth.ai>
To: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: ops-procurement@cardinalhealth.ai
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Observability add-ons: retention / tracing / dashboards — can we workshop options?

Hi Priyom,

Quick intro — I lead Platform at Cardinal Health. We’ve been evaluating Redwood for primary inference, and the engagement has gone well on throughput and latency. Before we move to a committed Dedicated deployment, the legal and finance teams want a clearer pricing and packaging proposal for observability add-ons (retention buckets, tracing ingestion, and the advanced dashboards pack).

We’re particularly focused on two things:
- How retention tiers map to predictable monthly charges for long-tail logs/metrics (we have a 90–180 day requirement for a subset of traces)
- Tracing ingestion vs. sampled tracing and what that means for unit economics
- Advanced dashboards: seat / dashboard / query pricing and whether there’s a pilot/seat credit for evaluation

Would Redwood run a short workshop with our infra and procurement leads to walk through a concrete pricing workbench? Ideal outcome: a one-page commercial path (pilot credits → committed slab → ramp concession). We can share sample telemetry volumes ahead of time.

Attachments: sample-ingest-summary.csv (small sample)

Proposed times (Pacific): Thu Nov 19 10:00–11:00, Fri Nov 20 08:00–09:00.

Thanks — happy to loop in legal/procurement once we have a shape.

— Elena
Elena Morris
VP Platform Engineering, Cardinal Health


From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morris <elena.morris@cardinalhealth.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 10:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Observability add-ons: retention / tracing / dashboards — can we workshop options?

Elena — thanks for the clear ask and the sample. We’re glad the inference side has been hitting your latency targets. Happy to run a focused pricing workbench with your infra + procurement folks.

Proposed agenda (30–45m):
- 5m: goals and success criteria for Cardinal Health (procurement asks)
- 10m: ingest/retention mapping and how retention buckets convert to monthly spend (examples using your sample)
- 10m: tracing cost levers (sampling, light vs. full spans, ingest vs. aggregate metrics)
- 10m: advanced dashboards commercial model (seats, dashboards, query units) and pilot credit options
- 5–10m: commercial path / concession ladder (pilot credits → time-limited credit → slab commit)

I’ve attached a pricing-workbench.xlsx that maps common telemetry profiles to nominal monthly costs and two concession paths we typically use for customers with regulated retention needs.

We can do Thu Nov 19 10:00 PT — I’ll hold that and send a calendar invite now. If procurement wants a slightly more commercial-first readout, Kimberly (pricing lead) is looped to propose concession bands. Jordan will cover the dashboards packaging. Rafael can speak to SLOs and how we can instrument sampling to preserve visibility while lowering cost.

Attachments: pricing-workbench.xlsx

Best,
Priyom Das
Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Elena Morris <elena.morris@cardinalhealth.ai>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 12:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: Observability add-ons: retention / tracing / dashboards — can we workshop options?

Priyom / team — quick commercial notes before the workshop tomorrow:

1) Concession framework (recommended):
   - Pilot: 60% consumption credit on observability add-ons for the first 30 days (to surface value and offset evaluation costs).
   - Commit: Tiered slab discounts at 6-month and 12-month commits; example: 15% off list for 6m, 25% off list for 12m on retention+tracing bundles when combined with Dedicated commit.
   - Guardrails: volume-based true-ups monthly; retention overages billed at 1.5x slab rate unless a transition window is agreed.

2) Dashboard packaging suggestion: move to a hybrid metric where the first 5 dashboards and up to 10 power-user seats are included in the bundle; additional dashboards billed per-dashboard or via a capped query unit. This simplifies procurement asks around "seat counts."

I’ll be prepared to walk the concession ladder and talk through negotiation ranges. Jordan, can you bring the two dashboard mock-ups we discussed (KPI pack + Incident Triage view)?

title: Executive summary: proposed POC timeline & success criteria — Arborlytics
preview:
From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aisha Patel <aisha.patel@arborlytics.com>, Tom Wu <tom.wu@arborlytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 14 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Executive summary: proposed POC timeline & success criteria — Arborlytics

Hi Aisha / Tom,

Thanks again for your time today — really appreciated the deep-dive on your document ingestion and search use case. I promised to follow up with a concise POC proposal so your team can quickly review and align internally. Below is a short executive summary + concrete next steps.

Goal (what success looks like)
- Demonstrate 95%+ QA relevance for the top-3 business queries you shared in the call ("contract lookup", "policy extraction", "claim summarization").
- End-to-end latency below 600ms p95 for single-question lookups in your test environment.
- Throughput: sustain ~40 qps for batched evaluation without degradation in relevance.

Proposed POC scope & timeline
- Week 0 (kickoff & access): data access, SSO/API keys, and installation of our lightweight connector. (Owners: Arborlytics engineering + Rafael)
- Week 1 (ingest & baseline): ingest 50k representative docs, run baseline indexing, and initial quality checks. (Owners: Rafael + Arborlytics eng)
- Week 2 (tuning & evaluations): run 3 evaluation passes, iterate prompt templates and quantization profile; measure p95 latency and relevance. (Owners: Marissa + Arborlytics PM)
- Week 3 (handoff & recommendations): delivery of evaluation report, production-readiness checklist, and cost/latency recommendations.

Required success criteria (for go/no-go)
1) Relevance: mean reciprocal rank (MRR) for the three queries >= 0.7 on the holdout set.
2) Latency: p95 end-to-end per-query latency <= 600ms in your staging environment.
3) Reliability: connector survives long-running ingest (50k docs) without >1% error rate.

Immediate asks from Arborlytics
- Confirm you can schedule the kick-off for Week 0 (we're available Mon/Tue 11/16–11/17).
- Provide an initial sample dataset (or allow S3 access) and the contact for the engineering lead who will help with credentials.

Attachments included (short):
- arborlytics_eval_plan_v1.pdf — evaluation plan, test cases, and measurement matrix
- redwood_technical_checklist.xlsx — connector checklist and environment requirements
- poa_scope_matrix.csv — simple mapping of deliverables and owners

If this aligns, we'll send a calendar invite and a short tech-readout questionnaire to speed setup. One more note — HubSpot deal id RDW-2026-1982; I'll capture any changes there so your procurement/sourcing folks have a single reference.

Best,
Diego Fernandez
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
Diego: +1 (415) 555-0123
[Attachment: arborlytics_eval_plan_v1.pdf (PDF)]
[Attachment: redwood_technical_checklist.xlsx (XLSX)]

To: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Wu <tom.wu@arborlytics.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 08:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Executive summary: proposed POC timeline & success criteria — Arborlytics

Diego — thanks, this is very clear and helpful. We like the concrete success criteria. A few quick clarifying questions before we lock dates:

1) Connectivity: for Week 0, is the lightweight connector able to run in our VPC with outbound-only egress to S3? We prefer not to open inbound ports.
2) Data handling: can you confirm that attachments will be hashed and not stored in Redwood's hosted logs for the trial? We need that for our security review.
3) Stakeholders: I'll be the PM lead and Tom (cc'd) will be the engineering owner for credentials. We may also loop in our Head of InfoSec (Maya R.) if the data flow requires a policy review.

We can do the kick-off on Tue 11/17 at 10:00–10:30 PT or Wed 11/18 at 09:00–09:30 PT. I prefer Tue 11/17.

Also, please send a short tech checklist for our infra team — I'll route to Tom.

Thanks — Aisha

> 1) Connectivity: for Week 0, is the lightweight connector able to run in our VPC with outbound-only egress to S3? We prefer not to open inbound ports.

Yes — our connector supports outbound-only egress (S3 or HTTPS) and can be deployed as a container behind your NAT. We will not require inbound ports. Rafael will own the connector deploy and support the initial runbook.

> 2) Data handling: can you confirm that attachments will be hashed and not stored in Redwood's hosted logs for the trial? We need that for our security review.

Confirmed — during the POC we will only retain metadata and hashes; raw document bodies can be configured to never leave your VPC. We will provide a short SOC2-adjacent evidence packet for your InfoSec team.

> 3) Stakeholders: I'll be the PM lead and Tom (cc'd) will be the engineering owner for credentials. We may also loop in our Head of InfoSec (Maya R.) if the data flow requires a policy review.

Perfect. For the kickoff we'll request Tom and Maya (if needed).

Subject: Executive summary: proposed POC timeline & success criteria — Arborlytics

Hi Aisha / Tom,

Thanks again for your time today — really appreciated the deep-dive on your document ingestion and search use case. I promised to follow up with a concise POC proposal so your team can quickly review and align internally. Below is a short executive summary + concrete next steps.

Goal (what success looks like)
- Demonstrate 95%+ QA relevance for the top-3 business queries you shared in the call ("contract lookup", "policy extraction", "claim summarization").
- End-to-end latency below 600ms p95 for single-question lookups in your test environment.
- Throughput: sustain ~40 qps for batched evaluation without degradation in relevance.

Proposed POC scope & timeline
- Week 0 (kickoff & access): data access, SSO/API keys, and installation of our lightweight connector. (Owners: Arborlytics engineering + Rafael)
- Week 1 (ingest & baseline): ingest 50k representative docs, run baseline indexing, and initial quality checks. (Owners: Rafael + Arborlytics eng)
- Week 2 (tuning & evaluations): run 3 evaluation passes, iterate prompt templates and quantization profile; measure p95 latency and relevance. (Owners: Marissa + Arborlytics PM)
- Week 3 (handoff & recommendations): delivery of evaluation report, production-readiness checklist, and cost/latency recommendations.

Required success criteria (for go/no-go)
1) Relevance: mean reciprocal rank (MRR) for the three queries >= 0.7 on the holdout set.
2) Latency: p95 end-to-end per-query latency <= 600ms in your staging environment.
3) Reliability: connector survives long-running ingest (50k docs) without >1% error rate.

Immediate asks from Arborlytics
- Confirm you can schedule the kick-off for Week 0 (we're available Mon/Tue 11/16–11/17).
- Provide an initial sample dataset (or allow S3 access) and the contact for the engineering lead who will help with credentials.

Attachments included (short):
- arborlytics_eval_plan_v1.pdf — evaluation plan, test cases, and measurement matrix
- redwood_technical_checklist.xlsx — connector checklist and environment requirements
- poa_scope_matrix.csv — simple mapping of deliverables and owners

If this aligns, we'll send a calendar invite and a short tech-readout questionnaire to speed setup. One more note — HubSpot deal id RDW-2026-1982; I'll capture any changes there so your procurement/sourcing folks have a single reference.

Best,
Diego Fernandez
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

title: Re: LaunchPad bootcamp — additional cohort credits request
preview:
From: Oliver Grant <oliver@launchpad.co>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Cc: LaunchPad Partnerships <partnerships@launchpad.co>
Date: Fri, 6 Nov 2026 10:22:00 -0800
Subject: Request: additional credits for LaunchPad Winter Bootcamp cohort

Hi Evelyn,

Hope you’re well — we loved the Redwood demo last month. Quick ask: our Winter Bootcamp (starts Jan) has 40 early-stage startups that plan to run prompt-heavy experiments (summarization + fine-tuning-like evals). The standard LaunchPad promo (one $5k credit per startup) won’t cover the expected usage.

Can Redwood make a one-off exception and provide either:
- a pooled credit bucket for the cohort (preferred) or
- a larger per-startup credit top-up for our 10 selected teams?

We’ve attached a completed credit_request_form.pdf with estimated token counts and rough billing contacts. If there’s an approval process or a short MAU/billing term to sign, we can move quickly — cohort onboarding is time-sensitive.

Appreciate any flexibility.

Best,
Oliver Grant
Head of Programs, LaunchPad Accelerator
oliver@launchpad.co

Attachment: credit_request_form.pdf


From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Grant <oliver@launchpad.co>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Nov 2026 13:00:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: additional credits for LaunchPad Winter Bootcamp cohort

Hi Oliver — thanks for the details and the form.

Quick internal note: looping in Dev to estimate how much we can safely pool and Rafael/Marissa for commercial/finance sign-off.

Oliver — can you confirm two things so we can fast-track this:
1) A conservative per-startup monthly token estimate (e.g., 100k tokens/month) or expected pooled peak?
2) Billing contact and whether LaunchPad wants a single invoice to you or to be billed per startup?

If you’re open to a pooled bucket we can propose a 3-month pilot structure and a bookkeeping/tracking rule for usage per team.

Thanks — I’ll come back with a proposed structure by EOD Monday.

Cheers,
Evelyn Hart
Director, Strategic Partnerships — Redwood Inference
Evelyn@redwood.com

On Fri, Nov 6, 2026 at 10:22 AM Oliver Grant <oliver@launchpad.co> wrote:
> Hi Evelyn,
> 
> Hope you’re well — we loved the Redwood demo last month. Quick ask: our Winter Bootcamp...
> 
> Attachment: credit_request_form.pdf


From: Dev Patel <dev@redwood.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Nov 2026 16:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: technical sizing + packaging idea for LaunchPad cohort

Quick take after a skim of the form:
- If teams mostly use summarization + embeddings, a pooled bucket is more efficient (prefix/KV caching will help).
- For 40 teams with conservative 100k tokens/month each, ~4M tokens/month; 3-month pilot ~12M tokens.
- Implementation options:
  * Pooled credits on a single account ID (easier to track, single invoice)
  * Per-team top-ups with billing tags (more admin but simpler for LaunchPad to distribute)

Risks & constraints:
- Billing system supports pooled credits but finance needs an explicit externally-visible promo code if we do a discount on overages.
- We should limit the trial to a fixed period (90 days) and cap per-team to prevent a single team spiking cost.

title: Proposal: Staged courtesy adjustment & VAT routing for HelixHealth invoicing spike
preview:
From: Sarah Li <sarah.li@helixhealth.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: HelixHealth Billing <billing@helixhealth.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Unexpected ingestion spike on Sept invoice — request for credit + VAT note

Hi Monica,

We noticed a large unexpected increase on our Sept invoice (INV-2026-09-3389). Our engineering team traced the spike to a duplicated connector job that ingested roughly 1.2M extra tokens across three projects between 2026-09-12 and 2026-09-14. Attached is a CSV with per-job usage and timestamps.

Could Redwood apply a goodwill/service credit for the duplicated usage and advise how this will be handled from a VAT perspective for our EU entity? We are VAT-registered in Ireland (VAT IE1234567). If a credit is issued, we need the tax treatment documented so our accounting team can record it correctly.

We'd prefer a credit memo applied to our next invoice, but if a refund is necessary to reconcile local withholding we can discuss. Please let us know the next steps and any evidence you require.

Thanks,
Sarah
Sarah Li
Finance Manager, HelixHealth
attachment: usage-summary-2026-09.csv


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Li <sarah.li@helixhealth.com>
Cc: HelixHealth Billing <billing@helixhealth.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 11:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected ingestion spike on Sept invoice — request for credit + VAT note

Hi Sarah — thanks for the quick context and the CSV. I’m really sorry this slipped through — we take spikes like this seriously.

Quick plan:
- We can provisionally apply a courtesy credit for the duplicated ingestion while our usage audit runs.
- To route the VAT correctly we’ll need your Irish VAT number on file (you included it — thanks) and a copy of your VAT certificate if available.
- Please confirm whether you prefer (A) a credit memo applied to next invoice or (B) a one-time refund to the bank account on file. If refund, also confirm wire instructions and whether you need any withholding documentation from our side.

I’ll loop in Finance and our billing ops to process a provisional credit memo today — pending final audit we’ll adjust if necessary. Attaching the provisional memo once created.

Appreciate the patience — I’ll follow up with exact credit amounts once we confirm the duplicated lines.

Best,
Monica Patel
Account Director, Redwood Inference
attachment: (will attach provisional-credit-memo-when-issued.pdf)


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 4 Nov 2026 08:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: Unexpected ingestion spike on Sept invoice — provisional credit request (HelixHealth)

Team — forwarding HelixHealth's spike. Key facts: duplicated connector job added ~1.2M extra tokens across three projects (CSV attached). Customer asked for credit memo applied to next invoice and VAT routing guidance (they are IE VAT-registered).

Proposed approach:
- Apply a provisional service credit equal to the disputed token cost less 30% (to account for our baseline processing / retention) — propose a provisional credit of EUR 7,200 today, with any reconciliation after the audit.
- Issue a credit memo (preferred by the customer) rather than cash refund unless Finance flags need for refund due to local withholding.
- VAT: treat the credit as a reduction to taxable supply. Since HelixHealth is EU VAT-registered we should support reverse-charge documentation; request Priya to confirm what evidence Finance needs to annotate the credit memo.

Attachments to include in customer email: provisional-credit-memo-INV-2026-11-1472.pdf, usage-summary-2026-09.csv, copy of signed MPA if required. Rafael — can you approve provisional credit and confirm final posting instructions for accounting? David — please advise if refund routing (wire) is needed for Ireland and whether Redwood should issue any extra withholding paperwork. Priya — please add a Vault/Drive folder for Helix evidence and share link.

Thanks,
Monica

>> Forwarded message attached above from HelixHealth


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 4 Nov 2026 10:47:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Unexpected ingestion spike on Sept invoice — provisional credit request (HelixHealth)

Monica — approved. Please proceed with staged crediting as proposed with the following specifics: 
1) Post an immediate provisional credit memo for EUR 7,200 (reference: INV-2026-11-1472) and mark it as \"provisional - pending usage audit\" in memo notes.
2) Finance to hold the refundable remainder for 30 days while Audit completes; any additional adjustments will be posted to next invoice or issued as a separate credit note.
3) VAT handling: treat this as a net reduction to supply. Because HelixHealth is VAT-registered in Ireland, annotate the credit memo with their VAT ID and include reverse-charge reference. Priya — confirm the Drive folder for VAT evidence and attach the signed MPA (we have it on file; include copy).
4) If customer insists on wire refund, Finance must confirm requirements for Irish banking and the TDS/withholding stance; David please own that.

David — please produce an accounting journal for the provisional memo and confirm whether a cash refund will trigger additional tax reporting in Ireland. If none required, we will avoid refund and use credit memo per customer preference.

Thanks, Rafael
VP, Finance Operations
attachment: signed-mpa-helix-2025.pdf

To: Sarah Li <sarah.li@helixhealth.com>

Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 16:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected ingestion spike on Sept invoice — provisional credit applied (INV-2026-09-3389)

Hi Sarah — quick update and confirmation.

1) We have issued a provisional credit memo: provisional-credit-memo-INV-2026-11-1472.pdf for EUR 7,200. This is posted against your account and will appear on your next invoice as a line item.
2) VAT treatment: the credit memo is annotated with your VAT number (IE1234567) and the memo references the reverse-charge treatment. Please keep this memo for your VAT records.
3) Next steps: our usage audit is underway. If the audit finds additional duplicated lines we will reconcile within 30 days and post an additional credit or adjustment as required. If you prefer a cash refund instead of the credit memo, reply to this email and Finance will advise on wire requirements and any tax paperwork needed.

As an apology for the disruption, we’ve also added a one-time 60-minute service credit (applies to live support) to your account.

title: Semantic fit recap — model picks, prompt sets & drift watch
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Liao <ethan.liao@brightlane.ai>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Nov 2026 15:08:00 -0800
Subject: Semantic fit recap — quick model picks and artifacts

Thanks again for your time today, Ethan — appreciated the walkthrough of BrightLane's search use case and the corpus composition (approx. 120k docs, mix of short FAQs and longer reports). Key takeaways + recommended next steps below.

Call highlights
- Primary goal: improve semantic recall for Q&A / search across knowledge base + analyst reports (low tolerance for false positives on legal/regulatory docs).
- Latency constraint: ~150ms p50 target for internal-facing search; throughput ~60 qps at peak.
- Concern: historical drift (vocab changes quarterly), and heterogeneous doc lengths (50 tokens to 8k tokens).

Quick model recommendations (pilot phase)
- Candidate A (higher accuracy, higher cost): redwood-emb-2-large — 1536 dims; strong on semantic grouping and recall@10 in our bench.
- Candidate B (cost-first, smaller footprint): redwood-emb-1-medium — 1024 dims; comparable R@10 on short queries, cheaper for batching.
- Fallback: open-compat-embed-v1 (quantized 768) for long-tail archived docs where latency/cost trumps top-k accuracy.

Bench summary (attached + link)
- Attached: brightlane_embedding_benchmark_v1.csv (MRR, Recall@1/5/10, MAP for each model on your seed prompt set).
- Short summary: redwood-emb-2-large: Recall@10 = 0.86; redwood-emb-1-medium: Recall@10 = 0.78; open-compat-embed-v1: Recall@10 = 0.68.

Prompt / eval artifacts
- Shared prompt sets: prompt_sets_brightlane_v1.md (contains 400 labeled queries, canonical answers, and negative sampling rules). Link in thread: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcD-example-benchmarks
- We recommend keeping the prompt set as the canonical regression suite for model rollouts.

Drift & dimensionality notes
- We saw concept drift in your production sample (monthly cosine mean shift ≈ 0.04) — recommend monthly re-eval and a sentinel set of 200 queries flagged for stability checks.
- Dimensionality tradeoff: 1536 dims gives better cluster separability for longer docs; 1024 dims hits a better cost/throughput point. We'll include both in the pilot to measure incremental improvements vs. cost.

Proposed pilot (50k doc index)
- Timeline: 3 weeks (prep + run): week 1 ingest & indexing, week 2 eval + human review, week 3 fine-tune thresholds/ops.
- Deliverables: benchmark CSV, interactive dashboard, quality regression alerts tuned to your sentinel set, SLO proposal for latency/recall.

Next steps / asks from you
1) Confirm a 1-hour kickoff next week (I can send a calendar invite).
2) Share a sample export (5k docs) + labels for a small subset if available.
3) Procurement contact to loop in for potential PO (cc included).

Attachments: brightlane_embedding_benchmark_v1.csv, prompt_sets_brightlane_v1.md

—
Camila Reyes
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0183



From: Ethan Liao <ethan.liao@brightlane.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Cc: BrightLane Procurement <procurement@brightlane.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 09:42:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Semantic fit recap — quick model picks and artifacts

Thanks, Camila — very helpful summary. A couple quick clarifications before we lock the kickoff: 

1) Dimensionality / storage cost: can you ballpark monthly storage (and a rough per-100k-query cost) differential between 1536 vs 1024? We need that for procurement.
2) Drift monitoring: what does the sentinel workflow look like operationally? Who on Redwood manages the alerts vs. who we should own?

We can do a kickoff next Wed 11/10 at 10am PT if that works. Also looping procurement in case we want a short SOW to start the pilot.

—
Ethan Liao
Head of Search, BrightLane Analytics
ethan.liao@brightlane.ai | +1 (617) 444-2290

To: Ethan Liao <ethan.liao@brightlane.ai>

Ethan — great questions, answered inline below and attaching a draft SOW for the pilot (pilot-sow-draft.pdf).

1) Cost/size ballpark (very rough, depends on quantization and caching):
- 1536 dims (redwood-emb-2-large): storage ≈ 230GB per 100k vectors (float32); with 8-bit quantization ~40–60GB. Estimated per-100k-query inference cost (hosted) ≈ $0.35–$0.60 depending on batching and cache hit rate.
- 1024 dims (redwood-emb-1-medium): storage ≈ 150GB per 100k vectors (float32); quantized ~30–45GB. Per-100k-query inference cost ≈ $0.20–$0.40.
Notes: Redwood Optimize will suggest batching/caching knobs and expected unit-cost reductions (we can attach a modeled cost curve once we have query distribution).

2) Drift monitoring & ownership:
- Proposed split: Redwood sets up automated evaluation harness and alerting (monthly job) + dashboard; BrightLane owns the triage workflow and labeling for sentinel queries.
- Alerting thresholds: monthly mean cosine shift > 0.03 or Recall@10 drop > 0.06 triggers an incident.
- We will provision an initial sentinel set of 200 queries and a baseline snapshot; outputs will feed your PagerDuty/Slack channel (we can wire up a webhook).

3) Kickoff: Wed 11/10 10am PT works for our team. I'll send a calendar invite.

Attachments: pilot-sow-draft.pdf

Thanks — I will also loop Rafael and Ben for the technical orchestration and onboarding tasks.

—

From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Liao <ethan.liao@brightlane.ai>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Nov 2026 15:08:00 -0800
Subject: Semantic fit recap — quick model picks and artifacts

Thanks again for your time today, Ethan — appreciated the walkthrough of BrightLane's search use case and the corpus composition (approx. 120k docs, mix of short FAQs and longer reports). Key takeaways + recommended next steps below.

Call highlights
- Primary goal: improve semantic recall for Q&A / search across knowledge base + analyst reports (low tolerance for false positives on legal/regulatory docs).
- Latency constraint: ~150ms p50 target for internal-facing search; throughput ~60 qps at peak.
- Concern: historical drift (vocab changes quarterly), and heterogeneous doc lengths (50 tokens to 8k tokens).

Quick model recommendations (pilot phase)
- Candidate A (higher accuracy, higher cost): redwood-emb-2-large — 1536 dims; strong on semantic grouping and recall@10 in our bench.
- Candidate B (cost-first, smaller footprint): redwood-emb-1-medium — 1024 dims; comparable R@10 on short queries, cheaper for batching.
- Fallback: open-compat-embed-v1 (quantized 768) for long-tail archived docs where latency/cost trumps top-k accuracy.

Bench summary (attached + link)
- Attached: brightlane_embedding_benchmark_v1.csv (MRR, Recall@1/5/10, MAP for each model on your seed prompt set).
- Short summary: redwood-emb-2-large: Recall@10 = 0.86; redwood-emb-1-medium: Recall@10 = 0.78; open-compat-embed-v1: Recall@10 = 0.68.

Prompt / eval artifacts
- Shared prompt sets: prompt_sets_brightlane_v1.md (contains 400 labeled queries, canonical answers, and negative sampling rules). Link in thread: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcD-example-benchmarks
- We recommend keeping the prompt set as the canonical regression suite for model rollouts.

Drift & dimensionality notes
- We saw concept drift in your production sample (monthly cosine mean shift ≈ 0.04) — recommend monthly re-eval and a sentinel set of 200 queries flagged for stability checks.
- Dimensionality tradeoff: 1536 dims gives better cluster separability for longer docs; 1024 dims hits a better cost/throughput point. We'll include both in the pilot to measure incremental improvements vs. cost.

Proposed pilot (50k doc index)
- Timeline: 3 weeks (prep + run): week 1 ingest & indexing, week 2 eval + human review, week 3 fine-tune thresholds/ops.
- Deliverables: benchmark CSV, interactive dashboard, quality regression alerts tuned to your sentinel set, SLO proposal for latency/recall.

Next steps / asks from you
1) Confirm a 1-hour kickoff next week (I can send a calendar invite).
2) Share a sample export (5k docs) + labels for a small subset if available.
3) Procurement contact to loop in for potential PO (cc included).

Attachments: brightlane_embedding_benchmark_v1.csv, prompt_sets_brightlane_v1.md

—
Camila Reyes
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0183

title: Trial engagement playbook & onboarding nudge
preview:
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Elena Gomez <elena.gomez@novanta.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 28 Oct 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Discovery recap + proposed trial engagement playbook

Hi Elena — thanks again for the call earlier. Quick recap and a proposed lightweight playbook so we can keep momentum on the 4-week proof-of-concept (POC):

High-level goal (your ask):
- Validate Redwood inference integration for real-time user-facing summaries with sub-500ms tail latency and <8% hallucination rate on your internal test prompts.

Proposed success criteria (confirm if agreeable):
1) Latency: p95 <500ms on 512-token window with your sample traffic profile.
2) Quality: automated evals against your 300 prompt set show <=8% major-regression (false facts) and >=85% acceptable ratings by reviewers.
3) Stability: 48-hour sustained throughput run with no more than 2% error rate.

Suggested timeline (fast, 4-week playbook):
- Week 0 (this week): contract/NDAs + security questionnaire exchange.
- Week 1: infra onboarding + data/secret access (test keys, S3 samples) + install test harness.
- Week 2: baseline runs, model tuning & config (quantization/batching), begin automated evals.
- Week 3: run edge cases, human review, quality adjustments.
- Week 4: final acceptance run, handoff notes + SOW for production.

Ownership (proposed):
- Novanta: Elena (product owner), Jake (procurement).
- Redwood: Lauren (AE, program lead), Marissa (CSM, onboarding), Rafael (SE, tech lead).

Open asks / next steps from our side:
- Elena: confirm success criteria above and any legal/ procurement timing constraints.
- Jake/Procurement: confirm PO timeline or if you prefer a pilot MSA/POC addendum.
- Novanta infra: provide a 24-hour sample trace and a small S3 bucket with representative docs (non-PHI).

Attachments: I attached a first-draft trial-playbook for your review (trial-plan-draft.pdf).

If that looks good, I’ll send calendar invites for the 1:1 onboarding session with Rafael and Marissa next Tuesday.

Thanks — excited to get this rolling.

—
Lauren Bishop
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0123

- Data: our infra team will provide a 48-hour sample trace and an S3 path with sanitized documents by Monday EOD.

One addition to success criteria: business metric — 10% reduction in downstream summarization rework (estimated based on our baseline) during the POC evaluation window. If we hit the quality goals and show measurable rework reduction, that’ll speed up approval.

Subject: Shortfall on Oct GCP Marketplace payout (APEX account)

Hi Rafael —

We ran the monthly remittance reconciliation and found a ~22% shortfall on the Oct GCP Marketplace payout for Apex Analytics (account: APEX-001). The payout we received on 2026-11-01 is materially lower than the invoice/usage records we have.

Quick summary:
- Expected settlement gross: USD 1,240,832 (per invoice bundle attached)
- Amount paid: USD 967,240
- Difference: USD 273,592

We suspect either: a) marketplace withholding due to entity mapping (APEX EMEA vs APEX APAC), or b) a subset of metered SKUs were reclassified and priced differently at settlement.

I've attached our invoice bundle and a ledger snapshot. Can you help triage and escalate to GCP if needed? Also need VAT split and withholding explanation — this impacts our tax filings.

Subject: Re: Shortfall on Oct GCP Marketplace payout (APEX account)

Quick triage notes (rolled up from our payout ledger):

- I pulled the marketplace settlement for APEX-001 (GCP partner ID G-APEX-549). The gross settlement on our side matches the GCP remittance file for Oct 2026 (file: GCP_remit_20261031.csv).
- The discrepancy looks like: ~USD 275k missing from the "metered SKUs" bucket. Non-metered SKUs (SaaS subscription fees) reconcile within 0.3%.
- Two potential root causes:
  a) APAC entity mapping: some usage recorded under APEX and routed to CloudBroker (reseller) entity; CloudBroker's routing rules apply a 20% fee/withholding in certain territories.
  b) Meter id mismatch: a set of usage IDs show as "unattributed" in GCP at payout time, and those were excluded from the gross to net mapping.

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>

Date: Mon, 1 Nov 2026 16:44:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Shortfall on Oct GCP Marketplace payout (APEX account)

Follow-up / outcome after today's call:

Summary:
- CloudBroker confirmed that ~USD 268,214 of Apex metered usage was routed through their reseller flow for Oct 24-31 due to a project tagging mismatch; their reseller routing policy applied a 20% withholding/fee at settlement which explains the net shortfall on Apex's bank statement.
- GCP support confirmed there is no unattributed metered export gap on the partner remit file — the missing lines were present but attributed to CloudBroker's reseller entity.
- Action for Apex: request CloudBroker's itemized remittance showing gross->net deductions so Apex can claim VAT/GST where applicable (CloudBroker agreed to provide within 5 business days).
- Action for Redwood: we'll prepare a reconciliation memo and provide per-line mapping to Apex (Jonas to deliver CSV).

Outstanding items / next steps:
1) CloudBroker to send itemized remittance and VAT breakdown (owner: CloudBroker; ETA: 2026-11-06).
2) Jonas to publish matched/unmatched SKU report and per-usage CSV (owner: Jonas Weber; ETA: 2026-11-02 EOD).
3) Rafael/Marissa to draft a reconciliation memo for Apex to use in tax filings (ETA: 2026-11-03).

If CloudBroker fails to provide the remittance we will escalate to GCP partner ops and request a reallocation (this may trigger a retro pay adjustment).

Thanks everyone — I'll keep this thread for updates.

Regards,
Rafael Mendes
Head of Marketplace Partnerships | Redwood Inference
rafael@redwood.com

title: Pilot MAP: evaluation sprint -> MSA/DPA handover and order-form critical path
preview:
From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@novaresearch.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: Nina Patel <nina.patel@novaresearch.com>
Date: 2026-10-20T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Pilot MAP kickoff + procurement timeline (Novaresearch pilot)

Kimberly —

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. Novaresearch is excited to run the 6-week pilot. Before we lock dates on our side, procurement asked for a clear mutual action plan that calls out: evaluation milestones, acceptance criteria, MSA/DPA review checkpoints, and the order-form sign window.

High-level ask from procurement: they need a one-week procurement review slot and a PO issuance target of Nov 19 to hit our internal Q4 roll plan. Can Redwood provide an MAP with gating steps and any legal artifacts you expect to require (MSA redlines, DPA template, SOC2 evidence link)?

Two constraints from us:
- Security team needs a consolidated evidence index (SOC2 + encryption/KMS notes)
- Legal wants to understand any non-standard SLA/uptime commitments before they escalate redlines.

Can you share a draft MAP and an order-form sequencing recommendation by 10/23?

Thanks —
Liam
Director, Platform Partnerships
Novaresearch


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@novaresearch.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.inference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.inference.com>, Nina Patel <nina.patel@novaresearch.com>
Date: 2026-10-21T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot MAP kickoff + procurement timeline (Novaresearch pilot)

Hi Liam —

Appreciate the clarity. Attaching an initial MAP that maps the 6-week evaluation sprint to the legal/procurement gates we typically use. Quick notes:
- We propose a canary evaluation (weeks 1–2) with acceptance checkpoints at the end of week 3 (functional) and week 5 (performance).
- Legal gating: MSA review starts 10/26, target sign (if no material redlines) 11/12. DPA review can run in parallel with MSA and is usually a 5–7 business day loop.
- Order form: we recommend preparing the order form in draft now so procurement can review while legal works redlines — final signature window planned 11/15–11/19 to align with your PO timing.

I looped Rafael for legal items and Evelyn for onboarding/acceptance criteria. I can get a sanitized SOC2 evidence index and the standard DPA template by EOD 10/23.

Action items I propose:
1) Redwood: deliver MAP + evidence index (10/23) — Kimberly
2) Novaresearch: confirm PO approval cadence and any internal blackout dates (10/23) — Liam
3) Legal sync (short 30m) to align MSA redline handling (10/26) — Rafael/Kimberly/Liam

Attached: 20261021_MAP_v0.xlsx (draft).

Best,
Kimberly
Head of GTM Ops
Redwood Inference


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.inference.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference.com>, Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@novaresearch.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.inference.com>
Date: 2026-10-26T08:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot MAP kickoff + procurement timeline (Novaresearch pilot)

Kimberly / Liam —

Quick prep notes ahead of our sync:
- I reviewed the draft MAP. From Legal's perspective the critical items that often create sequencing slippage are: indemnity caps, data processing clause specifics in the DPA (international transfers), and any exclusivity/usage carve-outs. If Novaresearch has a preferred redline set, send it to me prior to 10/26 and I'll triage.
- We can typically commit to a 7–10 business day turnaround on standard MSA redlines (assuming no new obligations). If novel SLA language is requested, that will add review cycles.

Proposed legal cadence:
- 10/26: 30m triage (identify must-have redlines)
- 10/27–11/05: legal redline loop (aim for first consolidated markup by 10/30)
- 11/09: final redline reconciliation and signature-ready MSA/DPA (if limited revisions)

If procurement needs a condensed path we can prioritize order-form commercial terms for parallel signature and use a DocuSign envelope to finalize the order form first, then finish MSA/DPA as a backend T&Cs attachment. That model reduces procurement friction but requires internal approval for accepting order-form-first flow.

—Rafael
Senior Counsel, Contracts
Redwood Inference


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@novaresearch.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.inference.com>, Nina Patel <nina.patel@novaresearch.com>
Date: 2026-10-26T12:21:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot MAP kickoff + procurement timeline (Novaresearch pilot)

All —

Thanks Rafael, that cadence works. Per our internal ops constraints I'd like to avoid finalizing the order form before MSA/DPA if it introduces any non-standard obligations on Redwood's side. That said, we can agree to a provisional commercial term sheet + DocuSign envelope for the order form to allow procurement to lock a PO while legal finalizes T&Cs.

Attached is an updated MAP with milestone owners and acceptance tests called out. Key changes:
- Week 3 functional acceptance: documented test cases (owner: Evelyn)
- Week 5 perf acceptance: 95th percentile latency target and throughput smoke tests (owner: Kim)
- Procurement: draft order form ready 11/05, signature window 11/15–11/19.

Please confirm the PO target (11/19) is firm on your side. If Novaresearch needs earlier, we can look to compress the legal loop (Rafael, can we try to batch comments and aim for first consolidated mark by 10/30?).

Attachments: 20261026_MAP_v1_KP_comments.xlsx; security_evidence_index.txt

Thanks,
Kimberly


From: Nina Patel <nina.patel@novaresearch.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference.com>, Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@novaresearch.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.inference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.inference.com>
Date: 2026-10-27T10:07:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot MAP kickoff + procurement timeline (Novaresearch pilot)

Kimberly —

PO target of 11/19 is our goal. Two asks from procurement/security:
1) Can Redwood provide a single consolidated evidence link (we prefer a one-page index) rather than multiple attachments? It helps our compliance reviewers.
2) If legal redlines are expected on data residency or DPA clauses, please call those out upfront so procurement can route earlier.

We will commit to reviewing the draft order form the week of 11/08 and will aim to get a PO out by 11/19 provided there are no material T&C changes.

Thanks —
Nina
Sr. Procurement Manager
Novaresearch

Subject: Re: Pilot MAP kickoff + procurement timeline (Novaresearch pilot)

Kim / team —

11/19 PO target is firm from Novaresearch side. Procurement will prioritize the order form review 11/08–11/12 as long as we get the draft by 11/05. Security notes: consolidated evidence index is preferred — Nina will collect and confirm what additional encryption/KMS detail they need.

Rafael — thanks for offering to batch comments. If Redwood can deliver the first consolidated MSA markup by 10/30 as discussed, we should have room to reconcile and keep the 11/19 PO target.

title: Re: Payment sweep schedule, credit guardrail and VAT routing for Finwell
preview:
From: Emily Turner <emily.turner@finwellmedical.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Payment terms request — PO 9876 and VAT entity details

Hi Marissa,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our procurement team asked me to reach out with the PO attached (Finwell_PO_9876.pdf) and a request to move to Net45 for the first three invoices, then reassess. The rationale is our internal payment cycle for new vendors — approval runs on a 45-day cadence.

Two quick items we need to clarify before we sign:
- Can Redwood confirm a monthly automated sweep on the 1st of each month (UTC) for any outstanding balance?
- We need a clear credit ceiling for initial usage while we ramp procurement approvals (we're proposing a USD 150k cap).
- VAT: our EU subsidiary will be the paying entity for EU-region tokens. Can you confirm you can issue invoices to a Finwell EU VAT ID and separate the EU VAT line on invoices?

Attached is the PO and a short vendor onboarding checklist from procurement. If you can send an updated SOW with the Net45 provisional term and a billing cadence note, we can run it through legal and get DocuSign queued.

Appreciate it — happy to hop on a quick call if helpful.

Best,
Emily Turner
Head of Procurement
Finwell Medical
emily.turner@finwellmedical.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

-----
Attachment: Finwell_PO_9876.pdf (application/pdf)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
To: Emily Turner <emily.turner@finwellmedical.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Oct 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Payment terms request — PO 9876 and VAT entity details

Hi Emily — thanks for sending this over and for the clear asks. We can accommodate a provisional Net45 for the first three invoices with the conditions below. Summary proposal (high level):

- Payment cadence: monthly automated sweep on the 1st UTC for outstanding invoice balances.
- Upfront security: 30% of the first-month estimated invoice due at contract signature (applied to first invoice).
- Credit ceiling: provisionally approve USD 150,000 usage cap while procurement completes their internal approvals; credit cap subject to quarterly review.
- VAT handling: we will issue invoices to your EU subsidiary and break out VAT lines when you supply the EU VAT number and legal name (please confirm the exact entity name and VAT ID).
- SOW: I'll attach a revised SOW (Draft-SOW-Finwell-Redwood-v1.pdf) showing the Net45 provisional term and the 30% upfront. Legal will want the PO and the signed SOW to finalize.

Next steps I propose:
1) You confirm the EU entity name + VAT ID for invoicing.
2) Rafael to confirm the credit check and finalize the USD 150k cap and the mechanics of the automated sweep (payment method, ACH/credit card tokenization).
3) I will send the updated SOW for DocuSign once Rafael confirms.

Looping Rafael here to validate the credit and sweep mechanics.

Thanks —
Marissa Cole
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwoodinference.com
+1 (650) 555-0199

-----
Attachment: Draft-SOW-Finwell-Redwood-v1.pdf (application/pdf)


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>, Emily Turner <emily.turner@finwellmedical.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Oct 2026 08:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Payment terms request — PO 9876 and VAT entity details

Hi Emily/Marissa,

Quick update from finance after an initial credit review:
- Recommended initial credit ceiling: USD 150,000 (as requested).
- Required security: 30% upfront deposit (applied to month 1).
- Sweep mechanics: We can support an automated ACH sweep on the 1st UTC. For ACH we'll need your bank's remittance details and an ACH authorization form; for card vaulting we'd need a tokenization agreement. ACH is preferred for larger volumes.
- Invoicing cadence: monthly invoices issued in the first week of the month, net45 conditional for the first 3 invoices. If usage exceeds the cap, overnight notifications + temporary throttling/fallback to hosted rate limits until top-up or invoice payment clears.

VAT: We will invoice the EU entity and include an explicit VAT line provided you send the VAT ID and legal invoicing name. If the EU entity requires reverse-charge handling, we will mark the invoice accordingly but need a confirmation email that the entity assumes VAT responsibility.

If all good, we'll prepare an invoice for the 30% deposit and Rafael will share the ACH form and remittance instructions.

Thanks,
Rafael Mendes
Director, Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0147


From: Emily Turner <emily.turner@finwellmedical.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Finwell Procurement <procurement@finwellmedical.com>
Date: Sun, 25 Oct 2026 11:43:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Payment terms request — PO 9876 and VAT entity details

- Finwell agrees to the 30% upfront payment. Please send the invoice for deposit and ACH authorization form.
- We accept the provisional USD 150,000 credit ceiling. If we anticipate exceeding usage in month 2, we'll notify you 10 days in advance.
- EU VAT details (for invoicing):
  Entity name: Finwell Medical GmbH
  VAT ID: DE812345678

Regarding the sweep: we prefer the 1st UTC sweep. We'll provide bank remittance details in a secure doc once we receive the ACH form.

Can you please:
1) Issue the deposit invoice and ACH form.
2) Send the DocuSign SOW reflecting Net45 (first 3 invoices) and the 30% deposit line so we can sign.

If you want to schedule a 15-minute call to finalize the billing mechanics, I'm available Mon/Tue mornings PST.

Best regards,
Emily Turner
Head of Procurement
Finwell Medical
emily.turner@finwellmedical.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

title: Post-demo: unit economics, SLOs, and batching questions
preview:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Emily Zhao <emily.zhao@avery-robotics.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-02T14:05:00-07:00
Subject: Post-demo: unit economics, SLOs, and batching questions

Hi Emily —

Thanks again for taking the demo this morning. Quick recap and a few clarifying questions so we can put together a concrete pricing proposal and POC plan. Short bullets below.

What we covered:
- Avery’s primary use: multimodal assistant for factory floor engineers (short prompts / multi-turn chat).
- Typical reply size: 80–300 tokens; occasional longer explainers up to ~800.
- Current pain: unpredictable per-call latency and cost spikes during peak shift overlaps.

What we need from you to lock estimates:
1) Estimated monthly token volume (ranges are fine: e.g., 10–50M, 50–200M, 200–500M).
2) Target SLOs: p50 / p95 latency goals for interactive flows (examples: p50 < 120ms, p95 < 400ms).
3) Is streaming response required for UX (partial token streaming) or can you accept buffered responses?
4) Target unit economics: what is your internal target $/1M tokens (ballpark)?
5) Any hard constraints on data residency or deployment mode (hosted vs dedicated vs on-prem).

Initial guidance from our side: for the volumes you mentioned in the demo, there are two realistic paths:
- Hosted "Pro" tier with streaming on top: lowest time-to-value, predictable latency for medium volumes, estimated unit cost ~ $1,000–$1,200 per 1M tokens depending on model family and streaming overhead.
- Dedicated reserved capacity with batching optimizations: lower unit cost at steady volume (closer to $650–$800 per 1M tokens) but requires a 3–12 month commit and more lead time.

Rafael, Marcus — can you validate those ballparks and add any quick notes on expected p95 impact from batching vs streaming?

Emily — if you can reply with the items above, I’ll turn this into a pricing worksheet and recommend a POC configuration (canary + 2-week load window). I attached the pricing worksheet stub for your reference.

Proposed next step: 30-min follow-up on Oct 6 to agree POC scope and confirm any security checklist items. Does 10am PT work?

Thanks,
Marissa Cole
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0123

Attachment: redwood-pricing-worksheet.xlsx (Excel, 45KB)


From: Emily Zhao <emily.zhao@avery-robotics.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-02T16:38:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo: unit economics, SLOs, and batching questions

Marissa —

Thanks for the clear summary. Below are the answers from our side. Pulled together by me and our infra lead (cc'd internally on some follow-ups).

1) Estimated monthly tokens (steady-state range): 200M–500M tokens.
2) Latency SLOs: our product team would like interactive flows to hit p50 < 120ms and p95 < 400ms for typical responses (80–300 tokens). For longer responses (~800 tokens) p95 can be relaxed to ~800ms.
3) Streaming: preferred for user perceived latency on multi-turn flows. If batching gives material cost savings, we could consider partial buffering for less time-sensitive endpoints.
4) Target unit economics: ideally target $/1M tokens in the vicinity of $900–$1,000. Above $1,200 would be a hard stretch for our budget baseline.
5) Data residency: US-only for now; no on-prem required for the initial POC.

We can do the Oct 6 slot at 10am PT. Please invite our infra lead (I can add them once the calendar invite goes out).

EMILY ZHAO
Head of Product, Avery Robotics
emily.zhao@avery-robotics.com | (206) 555-0192
Avery Robotics


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Emily Zhao <emily.zhao@avery-robotics.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-03T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo: unit economics, SLOs, and batching questions

Emily / Marissa —

Thanks — useful numbers. Quick technical read on trade-offs and a couple of conservative unit-cost estimates to use in the proposal:

Summary of latency vs cost options
- Streaming (low batching):
  - UX: best perceived latency; tokens emitted to client as generated.
  - Cost: slightly higher per-token due to streaming overhead and less batching opportunity.
  - Expected p95 impact: for models under 1k context and under moderate concurrency, p95 for 80–300 token responses is typically within your 400ms target on our Pro tier in hosted mode.

- Batching (larger batch windows):
  - UX: adds minimal queuing delay but can significantly reduce per-token compute cost at high QPS.
  - Cost: can reduce unit economics by ~20–35% at steady high volume if you can tolerate an added 20–80ms queuing window.
  - Risk: if load is spiky (shift overlap) you need autoscaling headroom or fallbacks to avoid p95 regressions.

Ballpark unit-costs (model family/seq len assumptions):
- Hosted Pro (streaming, managed autoscale): ~$1,050 per 1M tokens (estimate for mixed short/medium replies).
- Dedicated reserved (batch-optimized, 3–12mo commit): ~$700 per 1M tokens with conservative batching profiles; could approach $600/1M at very steady 500M+ volumes and aggressive batching.

Recommendation for the POC:
- Run a 2-week canary on Hosted Pro with streaming enabled for the main interactive endpoint (matches UX requirement).
- Enable prefix caching for repeated prompts (we can show typical savings in the worksheet).
- In parallel, size a Dedicated footprint estimate for a 3-mo pilot if you want to validate deeper unit-cost gains.

If Avery confirms the 200–500M range and target ~$900–1,000/1M, we can propose a blended road map: start Hosted Pro (fast rollout + streaming), then evaluate a Dedicated commit after 6 weeks of usage data.

Marissa — happy to join the Oct 6 sync and run through a quick cost model and chart showing cost vs latency across tiers and batch windows.

—Rafael
Rafael Mendes
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
rafael@redwood.ai


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-04T11:20:00-07:00

I also want to float a 10% promotional credit for the pilot to help bridge initial budgeting concerns — can we offer that for a 6-week trial without manager sign-off? If not, I’ll loop in legal/finance. Deal id: RDW-2026-AVY-0042. HubSpot link attached.

Subject: Re: Post-demo: unit economics, SLOs, and batching questions

Marissa —

Thanks for the clear summary. Below are the answers from our side. Pulled together by me and our infra lead (cc'd internally on some follow-ups).

1) Estimated monthly tokens (steady-state range): 200M–500M tokens.
2) Latency SLOs: our product team would like interactive flows to hit p50 < 120ms and p95 < 400ms for typical responses (80–300 tokens). For longer responses (~800 tokens) p95 can be relaxed to ~800ms.
3) Streaming: preferred for user perceived latency on multi-turn flows. If batching gives material cost savings, we could consider partial buffering for less time-sensitive endpoints.
4) Target unit economics: ideally target $/1M tokens in the vicinity of $900–$1,000. Above $1,200 would be a hard stretch for our budget baseline.
5) Data residency: US-only for now; no on-prem required for the initial POC.

We can do the Oct 6 slot at 10am PT. Please invite our infra lead (I can add them once the calendar invite goes out).

EMILY ZHAO
Head of Product, Avery Robotics
emily.zhao@avery-robotics.com | (206) 555-0192
Avery Robotics


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Emily Zhao <emily.zhao@avery-robotics.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-03T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo: unit economics, SLOs, and batching questions

Emily / Marissa —

Thanks — useful numbers. Quick technical read on trade-offs and a couple of conservative unit-cost estimates to use in the proposal:

Summary of latency vs cost options
- Streaming (low batching):
  - UX: best perceived latency; tokens emitted to client as generated.
  - Cost: slightly higher per-token due to streaming overhead and less batching opportunity.
  - Expected p95 impact: for models under 1k context and under moderate concurrency, p95 for 80–300 token responses is typically within your 400ms target on our Pro tier in hosted mode.

- Batching (larger batch windows):
  - UX: adds minimal queuing delay but can significantly reduce per-token compute cost at high QPS.
  - Cost: can reduce unit economics by ~20–35% at steady high volume if you can tolerate an added 20–80ms queuing window.
  - Risk: if load is spiky (shift overlap) you need autoscaling headroom or fallbacks to avoid p95 regressions.

Ballpark unit-costs (model family/seq len assumptions):
- Hosted Pro (streaming, managed autoscale): ~$1,050 per 1M tokens (estimate for mixed short/medium replies).
- Dedicated reserved (batch-optimized, 3–12mo commit): ~$700 per 1M tokens with conservative batching profiles; could approach $600/1M at very steady 500M+ volumes and aggressive batching.

Recommendation for the POC:
- Run a 2-week canary on Hosted Pro with streaming enabled for the main interactive endpoint (matches UX requirement).
- Enable prefix caching for repeated prompts (we can show typical savings in the worksheet).
- In parallel, size a Dedicated footprint estimate for a 3-mo pilot if you want to validate deeper unit-cost gains.

If Avery confirms the 200–500M range and target ~$900–1,000/1M, we can propose a blended road map: start Hosted Pro (fast rollout + streaming), then evaluate a Dedicated commit after 6 weeks of usage data.

Marissa — happy to join the Oct 6 sync and run through a quick cost model and chart showing cost vs latency across tiers and batch windows.

—Rafael
Rafael Mendes
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
rafael@redwood.ai

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>

Subject: Re: Initial: Reciprocal testcase passport and onboarding

Thanks Kate — and appreciate the CSV. High level: Redwood is happy to proceed with a reciprocal, time-limited exchange. I put together a first-pass MAP and attached a timeline workbook (MAP-timeline-v2.xlsx) that maps deliverables, acceptance criteria, and the rollback window you requested. Summary below: 

1) MAP Milestones (proposed)
- Day 0: Receive BR sample passport + signed MPA addendum
- Day 1–2: Ingest into ephemeral sandbox; hash-only provenance stored in Redwood audit logs
- Day 3–9: BrightRetail runs acceptance tests against pinned model variant (we provide an instrumented eval route)
- Day 10–14: Final acceptance review and sign-off; if accepted, proceed to pilot; if rejected, mutual root-cause and revert plan

2) Data handling and takedown
- Data is stored in an ephemeral, customer-scoped sandbox (separate namespace). Raw files retained for up to 14 days by default, then destroyed.
- If BrightRetail flags PII or a sensitive item, Redwood will pause routing to the sandbox and delete source artifacts within 48 hours; audit entry created.

3) Usage constraints
- Samples may be used for inference-only evaluation inside the sandbox, not for model training or public redistribution.
- Outputs derived from testcases may be used for debugging and acceptance reporting but are subject to the same deletion schedule.

We also propose a short canary run (50k tokens) to validate latency/cost expectations before the full QA window. Legal — I put the MPA addendum draft in the Drive link below; any line items you need highlighted?

I attached the timeline. If that looks good we'll slot Rafael to coordinate onboarding and Grace to own the audit/takedown playbook.

Ben
Senior Solutions Lead
Redwood Inference

1) MAP Milestones (proposed)

- Day 1–2: Ingest into ephemeral sandbox; hash-only provenance stored in Redwood audit logs
- Day 3–9: BrightRetail runs acceptance tests against pinned model variant (we provide an instrumented eval route)

2) Data handling and takedown
- Data is stored in an ephemeral, customer-scoped sandbox (separate namespace). Raw files retained for up to 14 days by default, then destroyed.

3) Usage constraints
- Samples may be used for inference-only evaluation inside the sandbox, not for model training or public redistribution.
- Outputs derived from testcases may be used for debugging and acceptance reporting but are subject to the same deletion schedule.
