title: Clarify remittance path for multi-currency invoice & VAT reclaim
preview:
From: Sophie Lemaire <sophie.lemaire@valeriantech.co.uk>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Cc: Gavin O'Neill <gavin.oneill@valeriantech.co.uk>
Date: 2026-06-10T08:12:00Z
Subject: Question on paying VAL-3921 (GBP invoice) — FX, intermediary fees & VAT

Hi Neha,

We received Invoice VAL-3921 (GBP) and intend to pay from our EUR account. Before we proceed, a couple of quick clarifications:

1) Currency handling: Do you accept EUR remittances and will you convert on your side, or should we send GBP to your UK collection account? If you accept EUR, can you confirm the exact IBAN/beneficiary details and any reference we must include?

2) Intermediary bank fees: Our bank typically uses SHA by default and intermediaries sometimes deduct a portion (we've seen £15–£45 taken on similar amounts). Will Redwood accept a net-of-fees payment or do you require OUR (sender pays) so the invoice amount arrives in full? If you prefer OUR, please confirm the beneficiary instructions we should give to our bank.

3) VAT: We are a UK-registered business. For cross-border SaaS, is Redwood invoicing gross with UK VAT, or should we expect a VAT-exempt invoice with a reverse-charge note? If you need any company identifiers from us (VAT number, registration country), let me know.

Attached: copy of the invoice (PDF). Happy to loop in our payments team (HSBC) if that helps.

Thanks,
Sophie
Head of Finance — Valerian Technologies

Attachment: Invoice_VAL-3921.pdf


From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Lemaire <sophie.lemaire@valeriantech.co.uk>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-10T12:30:00Z
Subject: Re: Question on paying VAL-3921 (GBP invoice) — FX, intermediary fees & VAT

Hi Sophie — thanks for the clear note and the invoice. Quick answers below; copied Marissa from Finance for VAT confirmation and GL posting.

1) Currency handling
- We can accept either GBP or EUR. Preferred: GBP to our UK collection account to avoid an extra conversion step.
- If you must pay in EUR we can accept it to our EUR account; Redwood will not perform an internal FX on your behalf unless explicitly agreed. Please indicate which currency when initiating the transfer.

2) Intermediary fees
- Our standard is SHA (shared). That will result in deductions if intermediaries are used. If you need the invoice amount to arrive in full, please instruct your bank to send with OUR charges (sender pays).
- If you instruct YOUR bank to use OUR, they will likely pass through intermediary fees to you; otherwise the received amount may be short and we will reconcile shortpayments.
- I am attaching a one‑page SWIFT trace request template we use when an intermediary deduction occurs — it helps us identify which node took the fee.

3) VAT
- Can you confirm Valerian's UK VAT number here? If you are B2B and we do not have a UK VAT liability for this contract, we typically issue an invoice with a reverse‑charge note (VAT zero) for EU B2B. Post‑Brexit UK rules can differ — copying Marissa to confirm Redwood's VAT stance for UK customers.

Next steps:
- If you can send the payment instruction (currency chosen + OUR/SHA) we'll confirm expected arrival account.
- If any deduction happens, please share the SWIFT MT103 or payment trace and we'll follow up.

Thanks,
Neha Kapoor
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachment: SWIFT_trace_request_template.xlsx

On Wed, Jun 10, 2026 at 08:12 Sophie Lemaire <sophie.lemaire@valeriantech.co.uk> wrote:
> Hi Neha,
> ...


From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-11T09:00:00Z
Subject: FW: Question on paying VAL-3921 (GBP invoice) — FX, intermediary fees & VAT

Marissa / Rafael — forward from Valerian (external). They plan to pay from EUR and have asked about intermediary deductions and VAT treatment for a UK B2B. Can you confirm:

- Do we have a UK VAT registration that covers SaaS sales to UK companies for this contract? If not, can we issue a VAT-exempt invoice with a reverse-charge statement for B2B UK customers?
- If the customer sends a GBP payment to our UK collection IBAN but the amount arrives short due to intermediary fees, do we net the fees automatically or post a shortpayment and chase the customer? Any recommended language for Neha to give the customer to avoid reconciliation work?
- Accounting question for Rafael: GL code to use for net-of-fees adjustments vs. receivable differs — do you want me to accept OUR only if they elect it?

Also attaching the invoice and the SWIFT trace template Sophie may return if a deduction occurs. Suggest reply to external: ask them to send OUR if they want guaranteed net, otherwise advise they will likely see SHA deductions and should include MT103 if deductions happen.

Thanks — appreciate a quick call if easier.

Neha Kapoor
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Attachment: Invoice_VAL-3921.pdf
Attachment: SWIFT_trace_request_template.xlsx


From: Sophie Lemaire <sophie.lemaire@valeriantech.co.uk>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Cc: Gavin O'Neill <gavin.oneill@valeriantech.co.uk>, HSBC Payments <payments@hsbc.com>
Date: 2026-06-12T17:40:00Z
Subject: Re: Question on paying VAL-3921 (GBP invoice) — FX, intermediary fees & VAT

Hi Neha — thanks for the clarity and for looping in Finance.

1) We will instruct our bank to send as OUR (we will absorb intermediary fees) so the invoice amount arrives in full. We'll use GBP to your UK collection account. Please confirm the exact payee name + IBAN you want used for GBP (copying your finance team would be helpful).

2) VAT: Our VAT number is GB123456789. We need Redwood to confirm whether you will issue a VAT-exempt invoice with a reverse-charge note for this transaction. If you cannot provide a reverse-charge invoice, we will need a VAT‑charged invoice to reclaim appropriately. Please confirm.

3) Timing & trace: Once the bank has submitted the payment we will send the SWIFT MT103/confirmation (HSBC will provide). If for any reason there is an intermediary deduction, we'll send the trace so you can identify the node.

Please send a short confirmation message with: (a) final beneficiary/IBAN for GBP; (b) VAT treatment confirmation (reverse-charge OK); and (c) any reference we must include on the payment (invoice ID already).

Best,
Sophie Lemaire
Head of Finance — Valerian Technologies

- Can you confirm Valerian's UK VAT number here? If you are B2B and we do not have a UK VAT liability for this contract, we typically issue an invoice with a reverse‑charge note (VAT zero) for EU B2B. Post‑Brexit UK rules can differ — copying Marissa to confirm Redwood's VAT stance for UK customers.

Next steps:
- If you can send the payment instruction (currency chosen + OUR/SHA) we'll confirm expected arrival account.

Subject: HOLD: Gated delivery - crate ASN mismatch / missing appointment form

Hi team,

We received two inbound crates for Redwood Inference (ASN #RX-77821) but the manifest on-site doesn't match the shipment IDs in our system. Per Moscone inbound policy we cannot accept gated deliveries without a completed appointment form and matching packing list. We placed the shipment on a temporary hold.

Please send the corrected appointment form and updated manifest. If the truck will be reconsigned to our advance warehouse, we need that confirmation and carrier ETA.

Freight ops will release once paperwork is reconciled and appointment is scheduled.

— Moscone Freight Ops

title: Mutual Action Plan — POC networking & SSO prerequisites
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Daniel Park <daniel.park@finexa.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Mutual Action Plan — POC networking & SSO prerequisites

Hi Daniel / Sophie,

Thanks again for taking the discovery call earlier today. Per our conversation I drafted a concise Mutual Action Plan (MAP) fragment focused on the POC environment setup so we can unblock the hands-on validation. Please review and confirm ownership/timing for the items below.

High-level POC milestone (target): Provision customer-isolated POC environment + connectivity and auth configured so Finexa devs can run sample inference workloads
Target completion date: 2026-06-18 (suggested)

MAP items / responsibilities:
- VPC peering / connectivity: Finexa network team to open a peering request and provide VPC CIDR and preferred peering method (AWS account ID vs Transit Gateway). Redwood to provision test cluster and provide egress IPs and reachability tests.
- IP allowlists / firewall rules: Finexa to whitelist Redwood egress ranges; Redwood will provide a short list of IPs and ports.
- SSO / SAML: Finexa identity team to share SAML metadata (IdP metadata XML) and a test user account (email + role). Redwood to configure SP metadata and run an SP-initiated test.
- Secrets / KMS: Decide if Finexa will use customer-managed KMS (BYOK) or Redwood-managed keys. If BYOK, Finexa to provide KMS key ARN and IAM role/principal for Redwood.
- Security review: Finexa security to confirm data classification and any required audit logs / retention settings.

Dependencies / blockers:
- Legal / procurement sign-off for PII-handling (if applicable)
- Network maintenance windows (please flag any black-out dates)
- Access to test credentials and KMS cross-account role (if BYOK)

Attachments: MAP_v0.1.xlsx (spreadsheet with milestone table), network-diagram.png (simple topology)

Next steps:
1) Can you confirm owners for each bullet above and provide the VPC CIDR + preferred peering method by EOD Thu 21 May?
2) If Finexa prefers BYOK, please send the KMS key ARN and a proposed IAM role/principal we can assume for the POC.
3) I'll circulate an updated MAP and a short runbook for the connectivity test once we have the CIDR.

Thanks —
Camila Reyes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0124

Attachment: MAP_v0.1.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)
Attachment: network-diagram.png (image/png)

From: Daniel Park <daniel.park@finexa.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Cc: Sophie Lim <sophie.lim@finexa.com>, netops@finexa.com
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 09:27:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Mutual Action Plan — POC networking & SSO prerequisites

Camila —

Thanks for the clear summary. A few quick notes from our side: we can own the VPC peering work. The POC VPC CIDR will be 10.10.0.0/16 (we will confirm exact subnets once netops creates the peering request).

A couple of clarifications:
- Peering method: we'd prefer doing a Transit Gateway attachment if Redwood supports it; otherwise direct VPC peering is fine.
- SSO: Finexa uses Azure AD; we will provide IdP metadata and a test user (sophie.lim@finexa.com) with the DevOps role for SP-initiated testing.
- KMS: We would prefer BYOK (customer-managed key) for the POC if Redwood can assume a cross-account role. Please confirm compatibility and any minimum permissions needed.

Security will need to review the MAP doc; I attached a contact list for our security and network owners. We also have a planned maintenance window next Saturday (23 May) that may affect early connectivity — netops will flag windows in the peering request.

I’ll aim to get the metadata + KMS ARN to you by Friday.

Thanks,
Daniel Park
Director, Platform Engineering — Finexa Health
Attachment: Finexa_Security_Contacts.pdf (application/pdf)

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Cc: Daniel Park <daniel.park@finexa.com>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Mutual Action Plan — POC networking & SSO prerequisites

Thanks for the note — cc'ing Daniel so we keep the thread together. A few technical items from the deployment side: 

1) Network/ports: For the POC we only need outbound HTTPS (443) from the Redwood workers to Finexa endpoints. If you plan to allow only specific ports, please ensure 443 outbound is open.

2) Egress IPs: I'll provide a short list of egress IP ranges (and an explicit 5-IP sample set for initial allowlist testing) by EOD today.

3) Transit Gateway vs direct peering: We support both. Transit Gateway is preferred for multi-account setups (less routing maintenance). If Finexa chooses TGW, we'll need the TGW ID and attachment request details.

4) SAML: We support Azure AD SAML with SP-initiated flow. Please send IdP metadata XML and a test user. For access mapping, we expect a claim with group or role attribute.

5) KMS/BYOK: We support customer-managed keys in AWS KMS. Required artifacts: KMS key ARN + a cross-account IAM role ARN for Redwood to assume with kms:Decrypt and kms:GenerateDataKey permissions for the POC. If Finexa prefers Redwood-managed keys, we can proceed immediately with documented encryption-at-rest controls.

6) Timeline: Given Finexa's maintenance window on 23 May, we should move the target completion to 2026-06-22 to leave room for testing and any security reviews.

I'll attach a short note on KMS integration patterns.

— Rafael
Technical Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
rafael@redwood.com

Attachment: redwood-kms-integration.md (text/markdown)

title: Re: Request for cryptographic controls evidence (SIG/CAIQ)
preview:
From: Marta Lopez <marta.lopez@bluebridgehealth.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 16 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: cryptographic controls evidence for SIG/CAIQ

Hi Neha,

We're wrapping up the vendor SIG/CAIQ review for Bluebridge Health and need a focused set of artifacts around encryption and key management. Specifically we need: 
- A short diagram or description of how KMS is used for at-rest encryption across hosted vs dedicated deployments
- Confirmation of HSM usage for key material (certificate chain or attestation) where applicable
- Key rotation policy (frequency, triggers, emergency rotation procedures)
- Clarification on customer-controlled keys vs Redwood-managed keys and any supported Bring-Your-Own-Key (BYOK) workflows

Can you confirm whether you can deliver these items by EOD Tues (2026-05-18)? We plan to attach them to the CAIQ answers (question group A2/A6). If you have a single zip or Drive folder, that's perfect.

Thanks,
Marta
Marta Lopez
Head of Vendor Risk
Bluebridge Health
security@bluebridgehealth.com

--
Note: we'll need a footprint/summary for the SIG question fields, too.

To: Marta Lopez <marta.lopez@bluebridgehealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 16 May 2026 11:05:00 -0700

Marta,

Thanks for the clear list — we can provide that. Looping in Rafael (Security) to assemble the artifacts and Monica (your account lead) so she can help prioritize with your procurement timeline.

Plan: Rafael will confirm what is public vs internal, I will coordinate packaging into a single Drive folder, and Monica will confirm acceptance criteria for the CAIQ import.

Target: We'll aim to deliver the Drive folder by EOD Tue (18 May). If we hit any scope questions we'll flag immediately.

Best,
Neha Kapoor
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
neha.kapoor@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0128

On Sat, May 16, 2026 at 09:12 AM Marta Lopez <marta.lopez@bluebridgehealth.com> wrote:
> We're wrapping up the vendor SIG/CAIQ review...

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Marta Lopez <marta.lopez@bluebridgehealth.com>
Date: Sun, 17 May 2026 10:00:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: cryptographic controls evidence for SIG/CAIQ

Neha/Monica — thanks for the tag. Quick notes from the security team: 

1) Scope distinction: Redwood offers both Redwood-managed keys (default) and Dedicated/Private deployments which can be configured to use customer-managed keys via KMS integrations or by importing keys into an HSM-backed keystore. Let Marta know which model the CAIQ review should assume (managed vs BYOK).

2) Artifacts we'll provide: 
   - High-level KMS architecture diagram (redwood_kms_architecture_diagram.pdf)
   - HSM attestation chain / certs (hsm_certificate_chain.pem) — redacted for internal IP, includes vendor attestation header
   - Key management & rotation policy (key_rotation_policy_v2.1.pdf) — includes rotation cadence and emergency key-compromise playbook
   - CSV mapping for SIG/CAIQ crypto questions (sig_ca_response.csv) — answers with links to artifacts

3) A couple of caveats: the HSM cert is allowed to be shared under NDA/contract; if Bluebridge needs full attestation docs we'll request an access agreement. The rotation policy contains a short section on custodian roles that we may redact for public distribution.

I'll assemble the folder and flag anything that requires legal/AM sign-off. Rafael.

--
On Sat, May 16, 2026 at 11:05 AM Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks for the clear list — we can provide that...

Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 15:23:00 -07:00

Marta — attaching the artifacts requested. See Drive folder link below and the included files. Rafael and I redacted a few internal-only items (noted in the README inside the folder) but everything required to answer the A2/A6 crypto questions is present. Please confirm receipt and whether the CSV mapping works for your CAIQ import.

Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-redwood-evidence (view link)

Attached files (in folder and zipped archive):
- redwood_kms_architecture_diagram.pdf (PNG-based diagram + brief narrative)
- hsm_certificate_chain.pem (PEM text; vendor attestation headers included)
- key_rotation_policy_v2.1.pdf (rotation cadence: monthly automatic rolling for data keys; annual and on-demand for master keys; emergency rekey playbook)
- sig_ca_response.csv (SIG/CAIQ question IDs mapped to artifact links and short answers)

Notes:
- For BYOK/Dedicated deployments we include integration notes and supported KMS providers (AWS KMS, GCP KMS, Azure Key Vault).
- We recommend citing the rotation cadence for data keys (30 days) in your CAIQ answers and linking to key_rotation_policy_v2.1.pdf for details.
- If Bluebridge needs a full HSM attestation package we'll coordinate an NDA scope via Legal — Rafael will follow up if requested.

Thanks — please ping me if you need any edits before ingestion.

Best,
Neha Kapoor
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
neha.kapoor@redwood.com

Subject: Re: Past due: INV-2026-0412 — 45 days overdue

Hi Markus,

Thanks for the reminder. Our AP is holding payment — the invoice you issued does not include a VAT line and our procurement policy requires either: (a) a VAT-inclusive invoice with breakdown, or (b) a confirmed reverse-charge note referencing our VAT ID. We also need the PO number on the invoice to match our system (PO #4589).

I've attached the PO we have on file. Can you re-issue the invoice with a VAT breakdown (we're in Spain, VAT 21%) or confirm reverse charge and provide the signed contract reference? Once we have that, payment will be scheduled.

Naomi, Rishi — forwarding the thread with MPA. They're withholding payment due to VAT/PO mismatch. Rafael (AE) is looped.

Why I am escalating:
- This invoice is material ($78k) and over 45 days past due.
- MPA is a strategic PLG customer; delaying too long risks churn on their expansion.
- They specifically require either a VAT-inclusive invoice or reverse-charge confirmation plus PO on file.

Proposed options for quick resolution (please advise):
1) Finance issues a revised invoice (INV-2026-0412-v2.pdf) with VAT line and PO #4589 and mark as ‘corrected’ — collection likely within 7 business days.
2) If reverse-charge applies, Legal signs a confirmation note and we reissue with reverse-charge reference — collection within 10 business days.
3) Offer a one-time 15-day net extension (no fees) contingent on a signed payment plan via DocuSign; Redwood absorbs small cashflow hit to preserve relationship.

Requested approvals:
- Rishi: permission to reissue corrected VAT invoice or route reverse-charge language (what's faster?)
- Naomi: sign-off on proposed one-time extension / exec outreach if needed (I can draft a short note).

Forwarded message (Mar 14):
> From: María Gomez <m.gomez@mpaventures.com>
> ...

Thanks,
Markus

- If legal needs to confirm reverse-charge instead, coordinate with Legal and notify me.

Rafael — please send a short executive note to MPA (below) once Finance confirms the corrected invoice and include the DocuSign payment plan link. I'll review the note before you send.

Executive note (draft):
> Maria — we appreciate your partnership. We’ve issued a corrected invoice with the VAT breakdown and included your PO. To expedite, we can provide a 15-day extension if you sign a short payment plan. Our Finance team confirms the corrected invoice will not affect your historic pricing.

Redwood Inference
